ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ta yoo, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ya, te ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ doŋ mɔ ya.
translated: When Jesus was teaching, he said, "I tell you, many will enter through the narrow gate into Paradise, but few will enter through the wide gate into Paradise."

ref: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
text: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ kɛɛ lee Gyeriko donɔɔ man nɛ, balaŋ burum sila wɔ.
translated: Jesus healed him when he came down to Jericho; the man saw his sins.

ref: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
text: Ɔ dana ɔ pampaa ɔ nyiŋmaa man waa mo feefu wa amɔla na waa lese amɔla bia ŋan do ɔ kpee man, na waa kuu wura ŋan ka do boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man. Yesu Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ Maake
translated: He came to the village and the people gathered around him, and he taught them many things and healed their sick compassionately. Jesus the Messiah said to Mark.

ref: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
text: Debokenaŋ man te ɔ kawee keŋ taŋawɔ, te ɔ nyiŋa ɔ wose.
translated: I spoke, and he did it with his hand, and he rested.

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ dɔɔ wɔle gyu wɔɔ keŋ man balinɔɔ weese nɛ kɔŋawɔ, te be tɔɔ wɔ kom ŋgba mena keŋ wɔɔte ŋon be yako nyi waa tɔ gyaŋgba nyibaŋ baŋ nɛ.
translated: Balaŋ went to the river to fetch water but fell in, and the people tried to rescue him but could not because the current was too strong.
